additionalAnnotations.description: Custom annotations to add into metadata

enableServiceLinks.description:
Enable service links in pods. Although enabled, mirroring k8s default, it is highly recommended to disable,
due to its legacy status [Legacy container links](https://docs.docker.com/engine/network/links/)

extraArgs.description: Extra KEDA Operator and Metrics Adapter container arguments

http.description:
The default HTTP timeout in milliseconds that KEDA should use
when making requests to external services. Removing this defaults to a
reasonable default

minTlsVersion.description: The minimum TLS version to use for all scalers that use raw HTTP clients (some scalers use SDKs to access target services. These have built-in HTTP clients, and this value does not necessarily apply to them)

repository.description: Image name of KEDA Metrics API Server

tag.description: Image tag of KEDA Metrics API Server. Optional, given app version of Helm chart is used by default

pullPolicy.description: Image pullPolicy for all KEDA components

Logging level for Metrics Server (Deprecated).
allowed values: `0` for info, `4` for debug, or an integer value greater than 0, specified as string

stderrthreshold.description:
Logging stderrthreshold for Metrics Server (Deprecated)
allowed values: 'DEBUG','INFO','WARN','ERROR','ALERT','EMERG'

zapEncoder.description:
Zap Logging encoder for Metrics Server.
allowed values: `json` or `console`

zapLevel.description:
Zap Logging level for Metrics Server.
allowed values: `debug`, `info`, `error`, or an integer value greater than 0, specified as string

zapTimeEncoding.description:
Zap logging time encoding for Metrics Server.
allowed values are `epoch`, `millis`, `nano`, `iso8601`, `rfc3339` or `rfc3339nano`

resources.description:
We provides the default values that we describe in our docs:
https://keda.sh/docs/latest/operate/cluster/
If you want to specify the resources (or totally remove the defaults), change or comment the following
lines, adjust them as necessary, or simply add the curly braces after 'operator' and/or 'metricServer'
and remove/comment the default values

metricServer.description: Manage [resource request & limits] of KEDA metrics apiserver pod

minTlsVersion.description: The minimum TLS version to use when KEDA components provide a TLS-enabled service.

portHttps.description: HTTPS port for KEDA Metric Server service

portHttpsTarget.description: HTTPS port for KEDA Metric Server container

automountServiceAccountToken.description: Specifies whether a service account should automount API-Credentials

name.description: The name of the service account to use.

metricsServer.description: [Pod Topology Constraints] of KEDA metrics apiserver pod

volumes.description: Extra volumes and volume mounts for the deployment. Optional.

extraVolumeMounts.description: Extra volume mounts for metric server deployment

extraVolumes.description: Extra volumes for metric server deployment

mountPath.description: Path where KEDA TLS certificates are mounted

secretName.description: Secret name to be mounted with KEDA TLS certificates

enableServiceLinks.description:
Enable service links in pods. Although enabled, mirroring k8s default, it is highly recommended to disable,
due to its legacy status [Legacy container links](https://docs.docker.com/engine/network/links/)

extraArgs.description: Extra KEDA Operator and Metrics Adapter container arguments

pullPolicy.description: Image pullPolicy for all KEDA components

tag.description: Image tag of KEDA admission-webhooks . Optional, given app version of Helm chart is used by default

format.description:
Logging format for KEDA Admission webhooks.
allowed values: `json` or `console`

level.description:
Logging level for KEDA Operator.
allowed values: `debug`, `info`, `error`, or an integer value greater than 0, specified as string

timeEncoding.description:
Logging time encoding for KEDA Operator.
allowed values are `epoch`, `millis`, `nano`, `iso8601`, `rfc3339` or `rfc3339nano`

webhooks.description: Pod labels for KEDA Admission webhooks
[Pod security context] of the KEDA admission webhooks

- alert: KedaScalerErrors
annotations:
description: Keda scaledObject {{ $labels.scaledObject }} is experiencing errors with {{ $labels.scaler }} scaler
summary: Keda Scaler {{ $labels.scaler }} Errors
expr: sum by ( scaledObject , scaler) (rate(keda_metrics_adapter_scaler_errors[2m]))  > 0
for: 2m
labels:

port.description: Port used for exposing KEDA admission webhooks prometheus metrics

allowAllServiceAccountTokenCreation.description: Allow Keda to access all Service Token for KEDA operator

serviceAccountTokenCreationRoles.description:
Creates roles and rolebindings from namespaced service accounts in the array which allow the KEDA operator
to request service account tokens for use with the boundServiceAccountToken trigger source.
If the namespace does not exist, this will cause the helm chart installation to fail.
- name: myServiceAccount
namespace: myServiceAccountNamespace

Allow Keda to access all Service Token for KEDA operator

namesAllowList.description:
Array of strings denoting what secrets the KEDA operator will be able to read, this takes into account
also the configured `watchNamespace`.
the default is an empty array -> no restriction on the secret name

maxUnavailable: 1

profiling.description:
This setting lets you enable profiling for all of the components of KEDA and in the specific port you choose
This can be useful when trying to investigate errors like memory leaks or CPU or even look at goroutines to understand better
This setting is disabled by default

port.description: Expose profiling on a specific port

interval.description: Interval at which metrics should be scraped If not specified Prometheus’ global scrape interval is used.

jobLabel.description: JobLabel selects the label from the associated Kubernetes service which will be used as the job label for all metrics. [ServiceMonitor Spec]

podTargetLabels.description: PodTargetLabels transfers labels on the Kubernetes `Pod` onto the created metrics

relabelings.description: List of expressions that define custom relabeling rules for metric server ServiceMonitor crd (prometheus operator). [RelabelConfig Spec]

scheme.description: HTTP scheme used for scraping. Defaults to `http`

scrapeTimeout.description: Timeout after which the scrape is ended If not specified, the Prometheus global scrape timeout is used unless it is less than Interval in which the latter is used

targetLabels.description: TargetLabels transfers labels from the Kubernetes `Service` onto the created metrics

targetPort.description: Name or number of the target port of the Pod behind the Service, the port must be specified with container port property. Mutually exclusive with port

App Protocol for service when scraping metrics endpoint
appProtocol: http

controlPlaneServiceAccountsNamespace.description:
Customize the namespace of k8s metrics-server deployment
This could also be achieved by the Kubernetes control plane manager flag --use-service-account-credentials:
[docs](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/command-line-tools-reference/kube-controller-manager/)

name.description: The name of the service account to use.

resources.description:
We provides the default values that we describe in our docs:
https://keda.sh/docs/latest/operate/cluster/
If you want to specify the resources (or totally remove the defaults), change or comment the following
lines, adjust them as necessary, or simply add the curly braces after 'operator' and/or 'metricServer'
and remove/comment the default values

metricServer.description: Manage [resource request & limits] of KEDA metrics apiserver pod

operator.description: Manage [resource request & limits] of KEDA operator pod

webhooks.description: Manage [resource request & limits] of KEDA admission webhooks pod

resources.description:
We provides the default values that we describe in our docs:
https://keda.sh/docs/latest/operate/cluster/
If you want to specify the resources (or totally remove the defaults), change or comment the following
lines, adjust them as necessary, or simply add the curly braces after 'operator' and/or 'metricServer'
and remove/comment the default values

webhooks.description: [Security context] of the admission webhooks container

annotations.description: Annotations to add the KEDA Metric Server service

watchNamespace.description: Defines Kubernetes namespaces to watch to scale their workloads. Default watches all namespaces

name.description: Name of the KEDA admission webhooks

additionalAnnotations.description: Custom annotations to add into metadata

additionalLabels.description: Custom labels to add into metadata

affinity.description:
[Affinity] for pod scheduling for KEDA operator, Metrics API Server and KEDA admission webhooks.
podAntiAffinity:
requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution:
- labelSelector:
matchExpressions:
- key: app
operator: In
values:
- keda-operator
- keda-operator-metrics-apiserver
- keda-admission-webhooks
topologyKey: "kubernetes.io/hostname"

asciiArt.description: Capability to turn on/off ASCII art in Helm installation notes

secretName.description: Secret name to be mounted with KEDA TLS certificates

autoGenerated.description: Enables the self generation for KEDA TLS certificates inside KEDA operator

caSecretName.description: Secret name where the CA is stored (generatedby cert-manager or user given)

renewBefore.description:
Certificate renewal time before expiration
8 months

issuer.description: Reference to custom Issuer. If issuer.generate is false, then issuer.group, issuer.kind and issuer.name are required

group.description: Custom Issuer group. Required when generate: false

kind.description: Custom Issuer kind. Required when generate: false

name.description: Custom Issuer name. Required when generate: false

caSecretName.description: Secret name where the CA is stored (generatedby cert-manager or user given)

enabled.description: Enables Cert-manager for certificate management

generateCA.description:
Generates a self-signed CA with Cert-manager.
If generateCA is false, the secret with the CA
has to be annotated with `cert-manager.io/allow-direct-injection: "true"`

issuer.description: Reference to custom Issuer. If issuer.generate is false, then issuer.group, issuer.kind and issuer.name are required

generate.description: Generates an Issuer resource with Cert-manager

group.description: Custom Issuer group. Required when generate: false

kind.description: Custom Issuer kind. Required when generate: false

name.description: Custom Issuer name. Required when generate: false

renewBefore.description:
Certificate renewal time before expiration
8 months

secretTemplate.description:
Add labels/annotations to secrets created by Certificate resources
[docs](https://cert-manager.io/docs/usage/certificate/#creating-certificate-resources)
annotations:
my-secret-annotation-1: "foo"
my-secret-annotation-2: "bar"
labels:
my-secret-label: foo

mountPath.description: Path where KEDA TLS certificates are mounted

secretName.description: Secret name to be mounted with KEDA TLS certificates

clusterName.description: Kubernetes cluster name. Used in features such as emitting CloudEvents

install.description: Defines whether the KEDA CRDs have to be installed or not.

customManagedBy.description: When specified, each rendered resource will have `app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: ${this}` label on it. Useful, when using only helm template with some other solution.

enableServiceLinks.description:
Enable service links in pods. Although enabled, mirroring k8s default, it is highly recommended to disable,
due to its legacy status [Legacy container links](https://docs.docker.com/engine/network/links/)

env.description:
Additional environment variables that will be passed onto all KEDA components
- name: ENV_NAME
value: 'ENV-VALUE'

extraArgs.description: Extra KEDA Operator and Metrics Adapter container arguments

extraObjects.description:
Array of extra K8s manifests to deploy
- apiVersion: keda.sh/v1alpha1
kind: ClusterTriggerAuthentication
metadata:
name: aws-credentials
namespace: keda
spec:
podIdentity:
provider: aws-eks

dnsConfig.description:
DNS config for KEDA components
nameservers:
- 1.2.3.4
searches:
- ns1.svc.cluster-domain.example
- my.dns.search.suffix
options:
- name: ndots
value: "1"
- name: attempts
value: "3"

registry.description: Global image registry of KEDA components

grpcTLSCertsSecret.description:
Set this if you are using an external scaler and want to communicate
over TLS (recommended). This variable holds the name of the secret that
will be mounted to the /grpccerts path on the Pod

hashiCorpVaultTLS.description:
Set this if you are using HashiCorp Vault and want to communicate
over TLS (recommended). This variable holds the name of the secret that
will be mounted to the /vault path on the Pod

http.description:
The default HTTP timeout in milliseconds that KEDA should use
when making requests to external services. Removing this defaults to a
reasonable default

enabled.description: Enable HTTP connection keep alive

minTlsVersion.description: The minimum TLS version to use for all scalers that use raw HTTP clients (some scalers use SDKs to access target services. These have built-in HTTP clients, and this value does not necessarily apply to them)

timeout.description: The default HTTP timeout to use for all scalers that use raw HTTP clients (some scalers use SDKs to access target services. These have built-in HTTP clients, and the timeout does not necessarily apply to them)

tlsCipherList.description: The list of cipher suites to use when making HTTP over TLS connections. When left empty or unset, the TLS implementation will provide a default list of cipher suites which are believed to be secure.

tag.description: Image tag of KEDA operator. Optional, given app version of Helm chart is used by default

imagePullSecrets.description: Name of secret to use to pull images to use to pull Docker images

disableCompression.description:
--Disable response compression for k8s restAPI in client-go.
Disabling compression simply means that turns off the process of making data smaller for K8s restAPI in client-go for faster transmission.
use ClusterFirstWithHostNet if `useHostNetwork: true` https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/dns-pod-service/#pod-s-dns-policy

dnsPolicy.description: Defined the DNS policy for the metric server

livenessProbe.description: Liveness probes for Metrics API Server ([docs](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/configure-liveness-readiness-startup-probes/))

readinessProbe.description: Readiness probes for Metrics API Server ([docs](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/configure-liveness-readiness-startup-probes/#define-readiness-probes))

replicaCount.description:
Capability to configure the number of replicas for KEDA metric server.
While you can run more replicas of our metric server, only one instance will used and serve traffic.
You can run multiple replicas, but they will not improve the performance of KEDA, it could only reduce downtime during a failover.
Learn more in [our documentation](https://keda.sh/docs/latest/operate/cluster/#high-availability).

revisionHistoryLimit.description: ReplicaSets for this Deployment you want to retain (Default: 10)

useHostNetwork.description: Enable metric server to use host network

affinity.description:
[Affinity] for pod scheduling for Metrics API Server. Takes precedence over the `affinity` field
podAntiAffinity:
requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution:
- labelSelector:
matchExpressions:
- key: app
operator: In
values:
- keda-operator-metrics-apiserver
topologyKey: "kubernetes.io/hostname"

disableCompression.description:
--Disable response compression for k8s restAPI in client-go.
Disabling compression simply means that turns off the process of making data smaller for K8s restAPI in client-go for faster transmission.
use ClusterFirstWithHostNet if `useHostNetwork: true` https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/dns-pod-service/#pod-s-dns-policy

dnsConfig.description:
DNS config for KEDA operator pod
use ClusterFirstWithHostNet if `useHostNetwork: true` https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/dns-pod-service/#pod-s-dns-policy

dnsPolicy.description: Defined the DNS policy for the operator

extraContainers.description:
Additional containers to run as part of the operator deployment
- name: hello-many
args:
- -c
- "while true; do echo hi; sleep 300; done"
command:
- /bin/sh
image: 'busybox:glibc'

extraInitContainers.description:
Additional init containers to run as part of the operator deployment
- name: hello-once
args:
- -c
- "echo 'Hello World!'"
command:
- /bin/sh
image: 'busybox:glibc'

replicaCount.description:
Capability to configure the number of replicas for KEDA operator.
While you can run more replicas of our operator, only one operator instance will be the leader and serving traffic.
You can run multiple replicas, but they will not improve the performance of KEDA, it could only reduce downtime during a failover.
Learn more in [our documentation](https://keda.sh/docs/latest/operate/cluster/#high-availability).

revisionHistoryLimit.description: ReplicaSets for this Deployment you want to retain (Default: 10)

useHostNetwork.description: Enable operator to use host network

secret.description: Restrict Secret Access for Metrics Server

allowAllServiceAccountTokenCreation.description: Allow Keda to access all Service Token for KEDA operator

namesAllowList.description:
Array of strings denoting what secrets the KEDA operator will be able to read, this takes into account
also the configured `watchNamespace`.
the default is an empty array -> no restriction on the secret name

secret.description:
Restrict Secret Access for KEDA operator
if true, KEDA operator will be able to read only secrets in {{ .Release.Namespace }} namespace

serviceAccountTokenCreationRoles.description:
Creates roles and rolebindings from namespaced service accounts in the array which allow the KEDA operator
to request service account tokens for use with the boundServiceAccountToken trigger source.
If the namespace does not exist, this will cause the helm chart installation to fail.
- name: myServiceAccount
namespace: myServiceAccountNamespace

metricsAdapter.description: Pod annotations for KEDA Metrics Adapter

webhooks.description: Pod annotations for KEDA Admission webhooks

metricServer.description:
Capability to configure [Pod Disruption Budget]
minAvailable: 1
maxUnavailable: 1

operator.description:
Capability to configure [Pod Disruption Budget]
minAvailable: 1
maxUnavailable: 1

webhooks.description:
Capability to configure [Pod Disruption Budget]
minAvailable: 1
maxUnavailable: 1

audience.description:
Sets the token audience for IRSA.
This will be set as an annotation on the KEDA service account.

enabled.description: Specifies whether [AWS IAM Roles for Service Accounts (IRSA)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/eks/latest/userguide/iam-roles-for-service-accounts.html) is to be enabled or not.

roleArn.description:
Set to the value of the ARN of an IAM role with a web identity provider.
This will be set as an annotation on the KEDA service account.

stsRegionalEndpoints.description:
Sets the use of an STS regional endpoint instead of global.
Recommended to use regional endpoint in almost all cases.
This will be set as an annotation on the KEDA service account.

tokenExpiration.description:
Set to the value of the service account token expiration duration.
This will be set as an annotation on the KEDA service account.

clientId.description: Id of Azure Active Directory Client to use for authentication with Azure Workload Identity. ([docs](https://keda.sh/docs/concepts/authentication/#azure-workload-identity))

enabled.description:
Set to true to enable Azure Workload Identity usage.
See https://keda.sh/docs/concepts/authentication/#azure-workload-identity
This will be set as a label on the KEDA service account.
Set to the value of the Azure Active Directory Client and Tenant Ids
respectively. These will be set as annotations on the KEDA service account.

tenantId.description:
Id Azure Active Directory Tenant to use for authentication with for Azure Workload Identity. ([docs](https://keda.sh/docs/concepts/authentication/#azure-workload-identity))
Set to the value of the service account token expiration duration.
This will be set as an annotation on the KEDA service account.

tokenExpiration.description: Duration in seconds to automatically expire tokens for the service account. ([docs](https://keda.sh/docs/concepts/authentication/#azure-workload-identity))

enabled.description:
Set to true to enable GCP Workload Identity.
See https://keda.sh/docs/2.10/authentication-providers/gcp-workload-identity/
This will be set as a annotation on the KEDA service account.

gcpIAMServiceAccount.description: GCP IAM Service Account Email which you would like to use for workload identity.

profiling.description:
This setting lets you enable profiling for all of the components of KEDA and in the specific port you choose
This can be useful when trying to investigate errors like memory leaks or CPU or even look at goroutines to understand better
This setting is disabled by default

enabled.description: Enable profiling for KEDA metrics server

port.description: Expose profiling on a specific port

App Protocol for service when scraping metrics endpoint
appProtocol: http

additionalLabels.description: Additional labels to add for metric server using ServiceMonitor crd (prometheus operator)

enabled.description: Enables ServiceMonitor creation for the Prometheus Operator

interval.description: Interval at which metrics should be scraped If not specified Prometheus’ global scrape interval is used.

jobLabel.description: JobLabel selects the label from the associated Kubernetes service which will be used as the job label for all metrics. [ServiceMonitor Spec]

metricRelabelings.description: List of expressions that define custom  metric relabeling rules for metric server ServiceMonitor crd after scrape has happened (prometheus operator). [RelabelConfig Spec]

port.description: Name of the service port this endpoint refers to. Mutually exclusive with targetPort

relabellings.description: DEPRECATED. List of expressions that define custom relabeling rules for metric server ServiceMonitor crd (prometheus operator). [RelabelConfig Spec]

scheme.description: HTTP scheme used for scraping. Defaults to `http`

scrapeTimeout.description: Timeout after which the scrape is ended If not specified, the Prometheus global scrape timeout is used unless it is less than Interval in which the latter is used

targetLabels.description: TargetLabels transfers labels from the Kubernetes `Service` onto the created metrics

targetPort.description: Name or number of the target port of the Pod behind the Service, the port must be specified with container port property. Mutually exclusive with port

webhooks.description:
- alert: KedaScalerErrors
annotations:
description: Keda scaledObject {{ $labels.scaledObject }} is experiencing errors with {{ $labels.scaler }} scaler
summary: Keda Scaler {{ $labels.scaler }} Errors
expr: sum by ( scaledObject , scaler) (rate(keda_metrics_adapter_scaler_errors[2m]))  > 0
for: 2m
labels:

enabled.description: Enable KEDA admission webhooks prometheus metrics expose

port.description: Port used for exposing KEDA admission webhooks prometheus metrics

additionalLabels.description: Additional labels to add for KEDA admission webhooks using prometheusRules crd (prometheus operator)

enabled.description: Enables PrometheusRules creation for the Prometheus Operator

namespace.description: Scraping namespace for KEDA admission webhooks using prometheusRules crd (prometheus operator)

scaledRefKinds.description:
List of custom resources that support the `scale` subresource and can be referenced by `scaledobject.spec.scaleTargetRef`.
The feature needs to be also enabled by `enabledCustomScaledRefKinds`.
If left empty, RBAC for `apiGroups: *` and `resources: *, */scale` will be created
note: Deployments and StatefulSets are supported out of the box

aggregateToDefaultRoles.description: Specifies whether RBAC for CRDs should be [aggregated](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/access-authn-authz/rbac/#aggregated-clusterroles) to default roles (view, edit, admin)

controlPlaneServiceAccountsNamespace.description:
Customize the namespace of k8s metrics-server deployment
This could also be achieved by the Kubernetes control plane manager flag --use-service-account-credentials:
[docs](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/command-line-tools-reference/kube-controller-manager/)

create.description: Specifies whether RBAC should be used

enabledCustomScaledRefKinds.description: Whether RBAC for configured CRDs that can have a `scale` subresource should be created

scaledRefKinds.description:
List of custom resources that support the `scale` subresource and can be referenced by `scaledobject.spec.scaleTargetRef`.
The feature needs to be also enabled by `enabledCustomScaledRefKinds`.
If left empty, RBAC for `apiGroups: *` and `resources: *, */scale` will be created
note: Deployments and StatefulSets are supported out of the box

metricServer.description: Manage [resource request & limits] of KEDA metrics apiserver pod

operator.description: Manage [resource request & limits] of KEDA operator pod

webhooks.description: Manage [resource request & limits] of KEDA admission webhooks pod

resources.description:
We provides the default values that we describe in our docs:
https://keda.sh/docs/latest/operate/cluster/
If you want to specify the resources (or totally remove the defaults), change or comment the following
lines, adjust them as necessary, or simply add the curly braces after 'operator' and/or 'metricServer'
and remove/comment the default values

webhooks.description: [Security context] of the admission webhooks container

annotations.description: Annotations to add the KEDA Metric Server service

minTlsVersion.description: The minimum TLS version to use when KEDA components provide a TLS-enabled service.

portHttps.description: HTTPS port for KEDA Metric Server service

portHttpsTarget.description: HTTPS port for KEDA Metric Server container

tlsCipherList.description: The list of cipher suites to use when KEDA components provide a TLS-enabled service. When left empty or unset, the TLS implementation will provide a default list of cipher suites which are believed to be secure.

annotations.description: Annotations to add to the service account

automountServiceAccountToken.description: Specifies whether a service account should automount API-Credentials

create.description: Specifies whether a service account should be created

name.description: The name of the service account to use.

annotations.description: Annotations to add to the service account

automountServiceAccountToken.description: Specifies whether a service account should automount API-Credentials

create.description: Specifies whether a service account should be created

name.description: The name of the service account to use.

automountServiceAccountToken.description: Specifies whether a service account should automount API-Credentials

create.description: Specifies whether a service account should be created

name.description: The name of the service account to use.

metricsServer.description: [Pod Topology Constraints] of KEDA metrics apiserver pod

operator.description: [Pod Topology Constraints] of KEDA operator pod

webhooks.description: [Pod Topology Constraints] of KEDA admission webhooks pod

metricsApiServer.description:
Capability to configure [Deployment upgrade strategy] for Metrics Api Server
type: RollingUpdate
rollingUpdate:
maxUnavailable: 1
maxSurge: 1

operator.description:
Capability to configure [Deployment upgrade strategy] for operator
type: RollingUpdate
rollingUpdate:
maxUnavailable: 1
maxSurge: 1

webhooks.description:
Capability to configure [Deployment upgrade strategy] for Admission webhooks
type: RollingUpdate
rollingUpdate:
maxUnavailable: 1
maxSurge: 1

volumes.description: Extra volumes and volume mounts for the deployment. Optional.

extraVolumeMounts.description: Extra volume mounts for KEDA deployment
Extra volume mounts for metric server deployment

extraVolumes.description: Extra volumes for metric server deployment

extraVolumeMounts.description: Extra volume mounts for admission webhooks deployment

extraVolumes.description: Extra volumes for admission webhooks deployment

watchNamespace.description: Defines Kubernetes namespaces to watch to scale their workloads. Default watches all namespaces

failurePolicy.description: [Failure policy](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/access-authn-authz/extensible-admission-controllers/#failure-policy) to use with KEDA admission webhooks

healthProbePort.description: Port number to use for KEDA admission webhooks health probe

name.description: Name of the KEDA admission webhooks

port.description: Port number to use for KEDA admission webhooks. Default is 9443.

replicaCount.description: Capability to configure the number of replicas for KEDA admission webhooks

revisionHistoryLimit.description: ReplicaSets for this Deployment you want to retain (Default: 10)

timeoutSeconds.description: Timeout in seconds for KEDA admission webhooks

useHostNetwork.description: Enable webhook to use host network, this is required on EKS with custom CNI

affinity.description:
[Affinity] for pod scheduling for KEDA admission webhooks. Takes precedence over the `affinity` field
podAntiAffinity:
requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution:
- labelSelector:
matchExpressions:
- key: app
operator: In
values:
- keda-admission-webhooks
topologyKey: "kubernetes.io/hostname"

dnsConfig.description: DNS config for KEDA admission webhooks pod

enabled.description: It also used to enable or disable webhook patching in the operator

failurePolicy.description: [Failure policy](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/access-authn-authz/extensible-admission-controllers/#failure-policy) to use with KEDA admission webhooks

healthProbePort.description: Port number to use for KEDA admission webhooks health probe

name.description: Name of the KEDA admission webhooks

port.description: Port number to use for KEDA admission webhooks. Default is 9443.

replicaCount.description: Capability to configure the number of replicas for KEDA admission webhooks

revisionHistoryLimit.description: ReplicaSets for this Deployment you want to retain (Default: 10)

timeoutSeconds.description: Timeout in seconds for KEDA admission webhooks

useHostNetwork.description: Enable webhook to use host network, this is required on EKS with custom CNI
